title: feat: add per-category blocking thresholds
body:
## Problem

Currently, blocking behavior is controlled by:
- `blockThreshold: "high"` - global threshold (critical, high, medium, low, none)
- `alwaysBlockCategories: ["security"]` - categories that block at ANY severity

There's no way to say "block security findings at medium+, but other categories at high+".

## Current Workarounds

| Option | Security Blocking | Other Categories |
|--------|-------------------|------------------|
| `alwaysBlockCategories: ["security"]` | All (including low) | high+ |
| Remove security from list | high+ | high+ |
| `blockThreshold: "medium"` | medium+ | medium+ (all categories) |

None provide fine-grained per-category control.

## Proposed Solution

Add `categoryThresholds` config:

```yaml
review:
  blockThreshold: "high"  # default for unlisted categories
  
  categoryThresholds:
    security: "medium"    # security blocks at medium+
    bug: "high"           # bugs block at high+ (same as default)
    # unlisted categories use blockThreshold
```

## Implementation Notes

- `categoryThresholds` overrides `blockThreshold` for specific categories
- `alwaysBlockCategories` still works (equivalent to threshold: "low")
- Categories not in `categoryThresholds` use the global `blockThreshold`

## Files to Modify

- `internal/config/config.go` - add `CategoryThresholds map[string]string`
- `internal/usecase/github/poster.go` or blocking logic - check per-category thresholds
- `bop.yaml` - document new option
